docs.title: Early Childhood Teaching Repository
docs.text:
Welcome to my Early Childhood Teaching Repository.

This website contains teaching routines, learning experiences, professional reflections and practical resources for early childhood education.

docs.title: Main Sections
docs.text:
• Toddler
  • Kinder

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
This folder sets up the mkdocs website content.

The index.md is the home page of the website

docs.title: 🌞 Morning Group Time (Before Morning Tea)
docs.text:
Purpose

  • Welcome children and build a sense of belonging
  • Establish daily routine
  • Support children's emotional wellbeing
  • Introduce the day's learning experiences

Suggested Duration 15–20 minutes

docs.title: Routine
docs.text:
Click to see Routine

1. Welcome Song

  • Children gather together and transition from play to group learning.
  • Children sit in the circle/ around the mat
  • Check in with 'Hello how are you song' by greeting the names of children and the teachers

Examples:

  • Hello how are you, hello how are you, hello how are you how are you today? It's nice to see (Name) here... ()
  • ...

2. Acknowledgement of Country

A respectful way to recognise Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and connection to Country.

Examples:

  • Daily Acknowledgement of Country (Essential)
  • singing inanay and tabanaba
  • Children's participation
  • Using visual resources
  • ...

3. Feelings Check-in

Children share how they feel.

Resources:

  • Emotion cards
  • Feelings chart
  • Puppet conversations

Questions:

  • How are you feeling today?
  • What makes you feel happy?
  • What can we do if we feel upset?

4. Weather, Seasonal, week, date, continent Discussion

Discuss:

  • Weather today (singing 'What's the weather song', then ask children to answer)
  • Season changes (singing 'season song', include nature observations, then ask children to answer)
  • Clothing choices (after discussing the season and weather)
  • Singing '7 days in a week song'
  • Singing 'continent song'
  • ...

6. Daily Plan (topic teaching) Introduction

Educator introduces (but do not do all the activvities at once):

  • Today's experiences
  • Special events (if applicable, see the education calendar of your state)
  • Teachers' expectations and Children's responsibilities

Example:

"Today we are going to investigate shadows outside."

7. Transition Song

Such as bee bee bumble bee. Children move to morning tea.

After morning tea, children either have free play or learning activities (assigned by teacher in small or big groups).

docs.title: ☀️ Midday Group Time (Before Lunch)
docs.text:
Purpose

  • Extend children's learning
  • Provide intentional teaching
  • Encourage discussion and inquiry
  • Consolidate children's experiences

Suggested Duration

15–25 minutes

docs.title: Routine
docs.text:
Click to see Routine

1. Pack up and Gathering Song

Signal children to transition into group time. - Pack away song - Let's make a circle song - Sit on the mat and clap

2. Review Morning Experiences

Discussion:

  • What did you play today?
  • What did you discover?
  • What questions do you have?

3. Intentional Teaching Experience

Possible areas:

Literacy

  • Story reading
  • Phonics awareness
  • Vocabulary development

Numeracy

  • Counting
  • Patterns
  • Measurement

STEM

  • Experiments
  • Predictions
  • Observations

Sustainability

  • Nature
  • Recycling
  • Gardening

First Nations Perspectives

  • Stories
  • Culture
  • Connection to Country

4. Open-ended Questions

Examples:

  • What do you think will happen?
  • Why do you think that?
  • How could we solve this problem?
  • What else could we try?

5. Transition to Lunch

  • Teacher ask names and send children to Wash hands 1 by 1.
  • Another Educator Prepare for lunch.

When lunch is finishing, educator puts on some calm music indicating the rest time.

docs.title: 🌿 Rest Time / Calming Experiences (After Lunch)
docs.text:
Purpose

  • Support children's wellbeing
  • Help children regulate emotions
  • Provide quiet recovery time

Possible Activities

  • Relaxing music
  • Story listening
  • Yoga
  • Breathing exercises
  • Mindfulness
  • Drawing
  • Quiet puzzles
  • books
  • Sensory activities
  • ...

After the noon rest, children either have quite free play or learning activities (assigned by teacher in small or big groups) until a group time before afternoon tea.

docs.title: 🌇 Afternoon Group Time (Before Afternoon Tea)
docs.text:
Purpose

  • Reflect on the day's learning experiences
  • Encourage children's voice and participation
  • Celebrate achievements and positive interactions
  • Strengthen relationships within the group
  • Support a calm transition to afternoon tea

Suggested Duration: 10–15 minutes (excluding pack away time)

Possible Resources

  • Reflection cards
  • Emotion cards
  • Puppets
  • Photos of today's learning
  • Children's work samples
  • Soft background music

Teaching Strategies

  • Active listening
  • Open-ended questioning
  • Positive reinforcement
  • Language scaffolding
  • Encouraging children's agency
  • Intentional teaching
Click to see Routine

Then students go for afternoon tea, after that, free play until the family come to pick up. When 4pm, families may come to pick up crowdedly, so can let all children go outdoor.

docs.title: 1. Pack Away
docs.text:
Children work together to tidy the learning environment before gathering for group time.

Learning Focus

  • Responsibility
  • Independence
  • Teamwork
  • Respect for the learning environment

Possible Strategies

  • Pack Away Song
  • Visual pack away cues
  • Small group responsibilities
  • Positive reinforcement

docs.title: 2. Gathering Song
docs.text:
Invite children to sit together and transition into group time.

Examples

  • Come and Sit Together
  • Hello Again Song
  • Circle Time Song

docs.title: 3. Reflection Circle
docs.text:
Invite children to share their experiences from the day. (photos can be shared)

Reflection Prompts

  • What was your favourite part of today?
  • What did you learn or discover?
  • What made you feel proud today?
  • What challenged you today?
  • What would you like to explore again tomorrow?

docs.title: 4. Celebrate Learning
docs.text:
Highlight children's learning, achievements and positive behaviours. (photos can be shared)

Examples

  • Artwork
  • Block constructions
  • Scientific discoveries
  • Creative ideas
  • Kindness moments
  • Teamwork
  • Problem-solving

Focus on effort, persistence and learning rather than outcomes.

Example

"Today I noticed Mia kept trying different ways to balance the blocks. She didn't give up."

docs.title: 5. Gratitude & Kindness
docs.text:
Encourage children to recognise positive social interactions. (teacher can share photos)

Discussion Prompts

  • Who helped you today?
  • How did you help someone today?
  • What kind thing did you do today?
  • What made someone smile today?
  • What are you thankful for today?

docs.title: 6. Mindful Moment
docs.text:
Finish the group time with a calming activity before afternoon tea.

Possible Activities

  • Belly breathing
  • Stretching
  • Mindful listening
  • Calm music
  • Guided relaxation
  • Quiet visualisation
  • animal bingo
  • other learning activities...

docs.title: 7. Transition to Afternoon Tea
docs.text:
Prepare children for the next part of the daily routine.

Transition Routine

  • Wash hands
  • Collect drink bottles
  • Line up calmly
  • Sing a transition song
  • Move to the dining area

docs.title: STEM Area
docs.text:
• Magnets
  • Light and shadow
  • Building challenges

docs.title: Creative Area
docs.text:
• Open-ended art
  • Clay
  • Collage

docs.title: Literacy Area
docs.text:
• Storytelling

  • Book making

docs.title: Construction Area
docs.text:
• Loose parts
  • Blocks
  • Engineering challenges

docs.title: Quiet Area
docs.text:
• Books
  • Puzzles
  • Mindfulness

docs.title: 🌇 Late Afternoon Group Time (Optional before home, usually when it is rainy and some children are picked up so late)
Routine
docs.text:
Click to see Routine

1. Welcome Back Song

Children transition from afternoon play.

2. Sharing Children's Learning

Educator can share:

  • Photos
  • Learning stories
  • Children's creations
  • Observations

Discussion:

  • Kindness
  • Friendship
  • Problem solving
  • Tomorrow's ideas

3. Story / Music / Goodbye Activity

Examples:

  • Favourite story
  • Songs
  • Puppet show
  • Goodbye song

docs.title: Daily Group Time Overview
docs.text: Time Group Time Main Purpose Morning Before Morning Tea Welcome & Daily Planning Midday Before Lunch Intentional Teaching & Inquiry Afternoon Before Afternoon Tea Reflection & Transition

docs.title: Australian ECT Annual Planning Calendar
docs.text:
For details, please refer to - Early Childhood Education and Care Calendar(2026 ECEC) - Key education dates

Here, as summarised, is A practical annual planning guide for Early Childhood Teachers (ECTs) in Victoria, Australia.

Click to see Routine

docs.text:
⭐ NAIDOC Week

One of the most important events in Australian Early Childhood Education.

docs.text:

  • Torres Strait Islander cultures

Note: Indigenous perspectives should be embedded respectfully throughout the year, not only during NAIDOC Week.

docs.title: 🌱 Year-round Learning Priorities
docs.text:
These learning areas are embedded throughout the year.

Learning Area Example Topics Literacy Storytelling, Phonics, Print Awareness Numeracy Counting, Patterns, Measurement STEM Science, Engineering, Nature, Inquiry Sustainability Recycling, Gardening, Composting Wellbeing Yoga, Mindfulness, Healthy Eating Outdoor Learning Loose Parts, Bush Kinder Indigenous Perspectives Acknowledgement of Country, Dreaming Stories, Native Plants Creative Arts Music, Dance, Drama Physical Development Gross Motor, Fine Motor

docs.title: Planning Tip
docs.text:
Experienced ECTs rarely plan around events alone.

Instead, they combine:

  • Children's interests
  • Intentional teaching
  • National events
  • EYLF outcomes
  • Family and community connections

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Children's Interest

  • Dinosaurs

National Event

  • National Science Week

Possible Learning Experiences

  • Dinosaur fossil excavation
  • Volcano experiment
  • Measuring dinosaur footprints
  • Clay fossils
  • Reading dinosaur books

docs.title: STEM Advantage for ECTs
docs.text:
If you have a STEM or Physics background, you can design engaging inquiry-based experiences such as:

  • Light & Shadow
  • Magnets
  • Sink & Float
  • Ramps & Motion
  • Simple Machines
  • Weather Observation
  • Sound & Vibration
  • Rainbows & Colours

These experiences align well with the EYLF inquiry approach and can become one of your strengths as an Early Childhood Teacher.

docs.title: Annual Planning Formula
docs.text:
A simple planning approach:

Children's Interests
        +
National Events
        +
Seasonal Changes
        +
Community Connections
        +
EYLF Outcomes
        ↓
Meaningful Learning Experiences

Remember: In Australian Early Childhood Education, children's interests always come first. Annual events enrich the curriculum, but they do not replace child-led, play-based learning.

docs.title: 📚 ECT Resource Websites (Australia)
docs.text: A curated collection of high-quality resources for Early Childhood Teachers (ECTs) in Australia, with a focus on Victoria.

docs.title: ⭐ Level 1 – Essential Resources (Use Frequently)
docs.text:
These are the websites every Australian ECT should know.

Website Purpose ACECQA EYLF, NQS, Regulations, Assessment & Rating, Qualifications Victorian Department of Education VEYLDF, Child Safe Standards, Inclusion, Transition to School Early Childhood Australia (ECA) Professional articles, pedagogy, webinars, best practice

docs.title: ⭐ Level 2 – Annual Planning & National Events
docs.text:
These websites provide yearly themes and free teaching resources.

Website Main Topics National Science Week STEM, inquiry learning, science experiments CBCA Book Week Children's literature, Book Week resources Harmony Week Diversity, multicultural education, inclusion NAIDOC Week Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander cultures

docs.title: ⭐ Level 3 – Teaching Resources
docs.text:
Useful for lesson planning and classroom materials.

Website Resource Type Twinkl Australia EYLF activities, worksheets, displays, games Teach Starter Lesson plans, posters, teaching resources SparkleBox Printable classroom resources

docs.title: ⭐ Level 4 – STEM Resources
docs.text:
Especially useful for teachers interested in science and inquiry learning.

Website Resource Type CSIRO Education STEM education, inquiry learning Questacon Science activities, STEM resources Little Scientists Australia STEM for children aged 0–6 years

docs.title: ⭐ Level 5 – First Nations Education
docs.text:
Highly recommended for embedding Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander perspectives.

Website Purpose Narragunnawali Indigenous education resources Reconciliation Australia Reconciliation and cultural understanding

docs.title: ⭐ Level 6 – Child Development & Assessment
docs.text:
Helpful for understanding children's development and supporting families.

Website Purpose Early Learning STEM Australia (ELSA) Play-based STEM learning Raising Children Network Child development, behaviour, parenting

docs.title: ⭐ Level 7 – Teaching Inspiration
docs.text:
These are not official resources but are widely used by educators.

  • Pinterest
  • YouTube
  • Instagram
  • Facebook ECT Groups

Typical workflow:

Official Websites
        ↓
Learn pedagogy and curriculum

Pinterest / Instagram
        ↓
Collect activity ideas

Own Reflection
        ↓
Create meaningful learning experiences

docs.title: Toddler Group Time
docs.text: Time of Day Suggested Length Main Purpose Best Activities Morning group time 10–15 min Settle, connect and introduce the day Welcome song, regulation, Acknowledgement of Country, movement song, short topic introduction Midday group time 5–10 min Calm down and prepare for lunch/rest Quiet song, breathing, short story, handwashing transition Afternoon group time 5–10 min Reconnect and review the day Gentle movement, reflection, favourite song, goodbye/transition song

docs.title: Toddler Morning group time (12-20min，core)
docs.text:
approximately 10–15 minutes, extended to 20 minutes depending on children’s engagement

Routine Time Activity Purpose Settle children * 2–3 min Instruction songs - “Let’s Make a Circle, circle, circle”- Sit on the mat and clap Help children join the group and understand group time is starting Emotional regulation * 2–3 min - Smell the flower, blow the flower- Banana unite song Support breathing, body awareness and emotional readiness Acknowledgement of Country * 2–3 min - Short oral acknowledgement (land,sky,friends,I)- Inanay song Build respectful daily routine Familiar movement song/or revise last topic 3–4 min - Sleeping Bunnies- Slippery Fish- Baby SharkChoose 1–2 songs only -or instead, can revise last topic Keep toddlers engaged through movement and rhythm/review and link to important prior concepts New topic introduction and intentional teaching 3–5 min - Use real objects, pictures, tools (resources related to activities, puppets)- Ask simple questions Introduce the learning focus in a concrete way Closing / transition * 1 min - See you later alligator song, or- encourage children to do the taught activity, or - ask names for washing hands before meals Signal the end and support a calm transition

"*" Those stars ones should be normally fixed for a well-structured routine

Ending - See you later alligator (30s)

Lyrics: See you later alligator, In a while crocodile, Give a hug ladybug, Blow a kiss jellyfish, See you soon big baboon, Out the door dinosaur, Take care polar bear, Wave goodbye butterfly. Group time is finished. Now we are going to play/do our activity

docs.title: Toddler Midday Group Time Routine
docs.text:
Midday group time: approximately 5–10 minutes, depending on children’s energy level, hunger and emotional readiness.

This group time should be calm, short and predictable. The main purpose is to help children transition from active play to lunch, rest time or quiet routines.

Routine Time Activity Purpose Gather and settle children ⭐ 1–2 min - Invite children to sit on the mat- Sing a short settling song- Example: “Open Shut Them” slow version, or the same as morning Help children calm their bodies and understand that play time is finishing Short calming song or breathing （can skip when children are tired and settled) 1–2 min - Smell the flower, blow the candle- Gentle hand actions- Slow clapping or quiet body movements Support emotional regulation before lunch or rest Short story / quiet book related to topic 3–5 min - Read a short picture book- Use a puppet or soft toy- Ask 1–2 simple questions only Encourage listening, language development and calm attention Lunch / rest time reminder ⭐ 1–2 min - Briefly explain what happens next- Example: “Now we will wash our hands, then have lunch.”- Remind children about simple expectations Prepare children for the next routine and reduce transition stress Transition song ⭐ 1 min - Sing a short transition song- Example: “Wash, Wash, Wash Your Hands”- Children move to handwashing or lunch Signal the end of group time and support a smooth transition

⭐ Starred routines should usually stay fixed to support a predictable and well-structured routine.

docs.title: Toddler Afternoon Group Time Routine
docs.text:
Afternoon group time: approximately 5–10 minutes, extended to 10–15 minutes only if children are highly engaged.

This group time should be relaxed and flexible. The main purpose is to help children reconnect after rest time, review the day and prepare for afternoon play or home time.

Routine Time Activity Purpose Gather children gently ⭐ 1–2 min - Invite children to the mat- Use a familiar song- Example: “Come and Sit Down” or “Let’s Make a Circle” Help children come together after rest, quiet play or afternoon routines Body wake-up / gentle movement 2–3 min - Stretch arms up high- Wiggle fingers- Touch head, shoulders, knees and toes slowly- Sing a gentle action song Support body awareness and help children re-engage after rest Reflection on the day 2–3 min - Show photos, objects or artwork from the day- Ask simple questions- Example: “What did we play today?” “Did you like painting?” Help children recall experiences and build oral language Short song / favourite song 2–3 min - Choose 1 familiar song- Examples: Slippery Fish, Sleeping Bunnies, Five Little Ducks- Keep it short and stop before children become too excited Support engagement, joy and group participation Goodbye / transition ⭐ 1–2 min - Sing “See You Later Alligator” or “Goodbye to Group Time”- Say what happens next- Example: “Now we are going outside” or “Now we are waiting for families.” Signal the end of group time and support a calm transition

⭐ Starred routines should usually stay fixed to support a predictable and well-structured routine.

docs.title: Topics in terms of teacher intention and children's interests
docs.text: Sometimes we run out of ideas. Here is a map for your reference to observe children's interests and develop your own teaching intention. Apart from this, I also recommend we can plan our activities in terms of EYLF (this webpage will be helpful) and education calender (such as victoria) in your states.

docs.title: Expectations of our room
docs.text:
Grouptime teaching ideas - Make a Tootoo Train and tour around the room we newly set up, talking about where the toys stay and how to pack up - Talk about the table setups - Repeat this tour to make sure children remember and be familiar with the learning environment

Activities and setups - N/A, just put plants, loose parts, or something beautiful on the tables

docs.title: Animal
docs.text:
Grouptime teaching ideas - sing and read Brown bear Brown bear what do you see - sing o macdonal had a farm with animal toys on hand, then make the farm with children while singing. Engage children by asking about animal sounds, and where to set the animal in the farm. - animal sounds

Activities and setups - toy animals and a big tree for animal farming

docs.title: Colour
docs.text:
Group time teaching ideas - match objects to the colour mat (colour papers and objects with different colours) -

Activities and setups - colour papers stick on the table and colourful objects such as Lego blocks in the middle

docs.title: Literacy
docs.text:
Group time teaching ideas - Counting - Sing '5 little monkeys jumping on the bed'. Use 5 little monkey puppets to visualise the drama and count with the children while seeing the monkeys become less and less. - Read sensory number books - Sing a counting fingers song. This is a toddler room, so remember to do it slowly, do not need to count to 10, 5 is good enough. - Bunny counting song for toddler and preschool (this is also finger play). - Shape - ... - Size - ...

Activities and setups - Counting - Teacher draws dots on hard papers, the challenge for children is to put the same number of clips or bonbons on the board. - Similarly, teacher draw dots and write numbers on the papers divided into grids. Teacher write the same numbers on the post-it notes. Toddlers sort them out. (see youtube)

docs.title: Audio library
docs.text: For toddler educational purpose songs, I found one nice Youtuber The Alana Banana Show - Educational Music Videos

docs.title: Indoor movement and emotional regulation
docs.text:
Songs - inanay - tabanaba - Fuzzy the clown (Demo video and Music version) - Open shut them - Sleeping bunny - Slippery fish - one little finger - bee bee bumble bee (before washing hands) - Rain, rain go away - itsy bitsy spider (demo video)

Listen to Stories - Three Little Pigs - The Very Hungry Caterpillar -

docs.text:
• Choochoowa
  • Hot potato
  • Freeze dance
  • Bear hunt
  • Baby Shark (crazy version)
  • Walking in the jungle
  • Head, shoulders, knees, and toes

docs.title: Activity library
docs.text:
• Sensory-colourwater
  • Sensory-Finger Painting (Warning, this is a messy play)
  • Literacy-Counting, Sorting, Shaping Loose Part in the Nature
